items.title: Somalia: Somali Opposition Group Urges Dialogue, Warns Against Interference in Puntland
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- The Somali Future Council congratulated Somalis on the 66th anniversary of the country's independence and unification, while warning that Somalia continues to face deepening political and security challenges.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somali Opposition Group Urges Dialogue, Warns Against Interference in Puntland

items.title: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu
items.description: The European Union is imposing new restrictions on visas for Somali nationals after a disagreement with Mogadishu over the return of irregular migrants to their home country.
items.titleIt: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu

items.title: How haircuts are helping men recover at a Kenyan mental health hospital
items.description: At Kenya’s largest mental health referral hospital, male patients are offered a different kind of therapy: a personal grooming session provided by two visiting barbers. Healthcare workers say personal grooming is an important aspect of recovery, as patients often lose interest in self-care.
items.titleIt: How haircuts are helping men recover at a Kenyan mental health hospital

items.title: Nigeria's Dangote refinery imports first crude oil from UAE
items.description: Nigeria’s Dangote Petroleum Refinery has imported two cargoes of crude oil from the United Arab Emirates this month, marking its first ever purchase from a Middle Eastern supplier, S&P Global Commodity Insights reported last week.
items.titleIt: Nigeria's Dangote refinery imports first crude oil from UAE

items.title: Somalia: Somalia Marks 66th Anniversary of Independence and Unification
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somali President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud joined a national flag-raising ceremony in Mogadishu at midnight on Tuesday to commemorate the 66th anniversary of the independence of the country's southern regions and their union with the north, which established the Somali Republic.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somalia Marks 66th Anniversary of Independence and Unification

items.title: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 30 GIU - The Ethiopian and Italian governments have signed a 70-million-euro financing package to support the macroeconomic reforms underway in Ethiopia and the country’s sustainable development agenda. The agreement was formally signed by Ethiopian Finance Minister Ahmed Shide and the Italian Ambassador to Ethiopia, Sem Fabrizi. The financing package, a testament to the strength and depth of the historic partnership between Ethiopia and Italy—founded on mutual trust and shared development priorities within the framework of the Mattei Plan for Africa— includes a €70 million c
items.titleIt: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development

items.title: Ambassador Nicoletti’s Mission to the Argentine Province of Catamarca
items.description: (ANSA) - BUENOS AIRES, 01 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Argentina, Fabrizio Nicoletti, undertook an institutional mission to the Province of Catamarca on June 29 and 30, with the aim of consolidating dialogue with provincial and municipal authorities and strengthening ties with the historic Italian community in the region. According to a press release, the visit represented an important opportunity to explore opportunities for collaboration between Italy and Catamarca in strategic sectors such as the mining industry, technological innovation, and sustainability. items.titleIt: Ambassador Nicoletti’s Mission to the Argentine Province of Catamarca

items.title: Sudan's RSF committed ethnic cleansing, Amnesty report alleges

items.title: Italian woman released in Egypt after Tajani intervenes
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 1 - Nessy Guerra, an Italian woman currently obliged to live in Egypt with her three-year-old daughter due to a custody battle with her estranged Egyptian husband, was released on Wednesday following an intervention by Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani after Egyptian police went to her Cairo home and took her to a station overnight, sources said. Guerra has returned home with her daughter, the sources said. The Egyptian police said Guerra had been detained in relation to an order by an Egyptian judge saying the father must have access to the child. Guerra received assistance f
items.titleIt: Italian woman released in Egypt after Tajani intervenes

items.title: Cairo embassy has requested pardon for Nessy Guerra says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 1 - Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Wednesday that Italy's embassy in Cairo has made a formal request to the Egyptian authorities for a presidential pardon for an adultery conviction for Nessy Guerra, an Italian woman currently obliged to live in Egypt with her three-year-old daughter due to a custody battle with her estranged Egyptian husband. During a question time session in the Lower House, the minister confirmed that he intervened personally, speaking to his Egyptian counterpart for Guerra's release after she was taken from her home in Cairo over
items.titleIt: Cairo embassy has requested pardon for Nessy Guerra says Tajani

items.title: In Sudan's Kordofan, a key city reels as paramilitary offensive looms
items.description: In a displacement camp near El-Obeid in Sudan's southern Kordofan region, Agsam Hamad trudges through searing heat to fetch murky water from a distant well, as paramilitary forces unleash their fiercest assault yet on the strategic city.

items.title: Working to bring Nessy Guerra and daughter back to Italy says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 1 - The government is working to make it possible for Nessy Guerra, an Italian woman currently obliged to live in Egypt with her three-year-old daughter due to a custody battle with her estranged Egyptian husband, to return to Italy, Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said on Wednesday. Tajani commented on the case via X following his intervention to have Guerra released after she was taken from her home in Cairo overnight and held at a police station. items.titleIt: Working to bring Nessy Guerra and daughter back to Italy says Tajani
